commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves provider-returned image-generation usage and response metadata for OpenAI-compatible, direct OpenAI, Google, and fal providers.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +63. Total +107 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main forwards ImageGenerationResult.metadata but the checked provider paths do not populate OpenAI-compatible usage, direct OpenAI usage, Google usageMetadata, or the selected fal response fields. I did not run live providers in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the additive plugin SDK metadata contract and normal exact-head merge gating, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Capture image-generation provider usage metadata
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve Google Gemini image `usageMetadata` in image result metadata when returned
- Preserve OpenAI/GPT Image 2 `usage` in direct and OpenAI-compatible image provider paths
- Preserve factual fal/Flux response metadata fields when returned, without synthesizing token usage

## Live payload validation
- MAI Image 2 generated successfully but returned no usage field, so local JSON helper now omits usage instead of emitting nulls
- Azure GPT Image 2 returned `usage`: input_tokens=19, output_tokens=196, total_tokens=215
- Azure FLUX.2-pro returned `created`, `data`, `request_meta`; no token usage

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Image-generation cost/usage metadata was being dropped. Google Gemini image usageMetadata, OpenAI/GPT Image usage, and provider-returned Flux metadata now flow into image result metadata when the API actually returns them.
- **Real environment tested:** GordieBox Mac Studio, local OpenClaw workspace and Azure image resources. Live payload validation used the configured Azure MAI Image 2, Azure GPT Image 2, and Azure FLUX.2-pro endpoints with real API responses; no secrets are included here.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  npm test -- --run src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/litellm/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/google/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/fal/image-generation-provider.test.ts
  CI=true pnpm tsgo:extensions
  python3 -m py_compile skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate.py skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_gpt.py skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_flux.py
  python3 -m py_compile repos/Gordie-OpenClaw/shared/skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate.py repos/Gordie-OpenClaw/shared/skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_gpt.py repos/Gordie-OpenClaw/shared/skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_flux.py
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live output from GordieBox/Azure:
  ```text
  Azure GPT Image 2 response usage -> input_tokens=19, output_tokens=196, total_tokens=215
  MAI Image 2 response keys -> data only; usage field absent
  Azure FLUX.2-pro response keys -> created, data, request_meta; usage field absent
  ```
  The patched OpenAI-compatible/direct OpenAI paths preserve the GPT Image 2 usage as `metadata.usage`. MAI helper JSON now omits usage when absent. The Flux path captures returned factual response metadata only.
- **Observed result after fix:** Returned usage is captured for Google/OpenAI-compatible/OpenAI when present, Flux records only actual provider-returned metadata, and MAI/Flux do not invent token counts when their live response omits usage. `CI=true pnpm tsgo:extensions` passed after the OpenAI response typing cleanup.
- **What was not tested:** No public FAL live call, because no FAL key is configured in this environment. Google live generation was not repeated for this PR because the known API contract returns `usageMetadata`; the patch is covered with provider tests and prior payload investigation.

## Validation
- `npm test -- --run src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/litellm/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/google/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/fal/image-generation-provider.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- Typechecked Vitest runs for modified provider tests: OpenAI, Google, fal, LiteLLM, OpenAI-compatible helper

Refs #85461
